slides.title: Dataforms in Kissflow Apps
slides.subtitle: Structured data storage, multiple views, and page components

slides.title: What is a Dataform?
bullets: A dataform is a structured data store within a Kissflow App
Captures and persists data independently of process workflows
Combines a form (schema) with views, reports, roles, and settings
Enterprise plan feature — not available on Basic plan
Use for master data, catalogs, inventories, registers, and lookups
Foundation for app pages — display and edit data through view components
slides.notes: Contrast dataforms with processes — dataforms are for persistent records, processes for workflow items.

slides.title: Creating a New Dataform
bullets: Quick create (+) on left navigation → Dataform
Three creation options: Build with AI, Create from scratch, Templates
Build with AI — describe your data model in natural language
Create from scratch — empty form you design field by field
Templates — install pre-built dataforms from Template gallery
Dataform must belong to an app — create or select app first
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > Creating and managing dataforms

slides.title: Dataform Editor Layout
bullets: Left panel: Form, Views, Reports, Roles, and Settings tabs
Center: form canvas where fields are arranged
Right panel: field palette — basic and advanced field types
Drag and drop fields onto the form or click to insert
Click Save to persist changes and make dataform available in app
Rename, duplicate, and archive dataforms from settings
slides.notes: Walk through the editor — mirror the process builder layout trainees already know.

slides.title: Dataform Field Types
bullets: Basic: Text, Number, Date, Dropdown, Checkbox, Email, Phone, URL
Advanced: Lookup, User, Attachment, Signature, Rich text, Grid
System fields: Created at, Created by, Modified at, Modified by, ID
Grid fields — tabular sub-data within a single record (no soft limit)
Button fields — up to 100 per dataform for custom actions
Rich text and Grid fields exempt from default form field soft limits
slides.notes: Explain soft limits — coordinate with team to avoid concurrent edit conflicts.

slides.title: Dataform Views — Overview
bullets: Views visualize collected dataform data in different formats
Three primary view types: Data table, Gallery, and Sheet
Each view has a unique name within the dataform
Views are added to app pages as draggable components
Configure filters, columns, sorting, and permissions per view
Multiple views of the same data — different perspectives for different roles
slides.notes: KEY CONCEPT — views are how end users interact with dataform data.

slides.title: Data Table View
bullets: Displays dataform records as rows with field columns as headers
Create: Dataform → Views → + New view → Data table → Create
Add columns from form fields or system fields (Select all option)
Sort by clicking column header or configuring default sort
Advanced data filters: Equal to, Not equal to, Part of, AND/OR conditions
Quick filters — separate tabs for saved filter combinations, drag to reorder

slides.title: Data Table — Advanced Configuration
bullets: Bulk actions — JavaScript code to act on selected or all rows
Add multiple bulk actions and reorder them as needed
Export data from data table view to downloadable file
Quick filters can be enabled/disabled at page component level
Column visibility and order controlled in view settings
Row selection — select all or specific rows for bulk operations
slides.notes: Demo bulk action — e.g., 'Mark selected as Inactive'.

slides.title: Gallery View
bullets: Card-based layout — ideal for visual catalogs and directories
Create: Views → + New view → Gallery → provide unique name
Card styles — up to five different card style templates
Layout options: Matrix, Horizontal, and Vertical arrangements
Card sizes: Small, Medium, Large, Extra large
Card borders: Gray border, Shadow, or None

slides.title: Gallery View — Sorting and Display
bullets: Custom sort — assign column field with ascending or descending order
Map form fields to card title, subtitle, image, and metadata
Apply data filters same as data table (Advanced filters)
Quick filters for tabbed filtered views
Best for: product catalogs, employee directories, asset galleries
Add gallery view component to app page via drag and drop
slides.notes: Show a product catalog example with images in cards.

slides.title: Sheet View
bullets: Grid/spreadsheet interface — edit data directly in cells
Column headings from selected form fields and system fields
Create: Views → + New view → Sheet → configure columns
Users add, modify, and delete rows at runtime
Add multiple rows using the Add button
Ideal for bulk data entry and spreadsheet-like editing
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > Sheet view in dataforms

slides.title: Sheet View — Page Component Settings
bullets: Drag sheet view component onto an app page
Show form — enable users with Manage permission to view/edit full form
Quick filter — turn on/off quick filters configured on the dataform
Export — enable CSV download button on the sheet view
Advanced data filters control which rows appear in the sheet
Runtime editing — authorized users modify data without opening individual forms
slides.notes: Contrast sheet view (editable grid) vs data table (read-focused with bulk actions).

slides.title: Configuring Field Permissions in Views
bullets: Field-level permissions differ per view and per role
Control which fields are visible, editable, or hidden in each view
Roles defined in dataform Roles tab map to app user groups
Manage permission — full CRUD access to records
View permission — read-only access to data
Configure per view so same data shows differently to different roles
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > Configuring field permissions in dataform views

slides.title: Data Filters — Advanced and Quick
bullets: Advanced filters: select field → operator → value/field/parameter
Operators: Equal to, Not equal to, Part of, Greater than, Less than, etc.
Combine conditions with AND/OR logic for complex rules
Quick filters — saved filter presets shown as separate tabs
Drag and drop to reorder quick filter tabs
Page-level override — enable/disable quick filters on embedded components
slides.notes: Demo: filter active employees where Department = 'Sales'.

slides.title: Importing and Exporting Data
bullets: Import data from CSV file into a dataform
Map CSV columns to dataform fields during import wizard
Export data from data table view or sheet view as CSV
Bulk import for initial data load during app setup
Validate data types during import to prevent errors
Coordinate imports with team — avoid concurrent unsaved edits
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > Importing data from CSV file

slides.title: Dataform Reports
bullets: Create reports from dataform data in the Reports tab
Same report builder as processes — charts, tables, pivots
Filter and aggregate dataform records for analytics
Embed dataform reports on app pages alongside views
Schedule report delivery to stakeholders
Combine dataform reports with process reports on dashboard pages
slides.notes: Dataforms have their own reports separate from views.

slides.title: Adding Dataform Views to App Pages
bullets: Open app page editor → drag Data table, Gallery, or Sheet component
Select the dataform and specific view to bind
Configure component settings: filters, export, show form button
Combine multiple view components on one page for different audiences
Use page-level scripting to interact with view components
Navigation menu links users to dataform-powered pages
slides.notes: Demo a complete app page with data table + gallery side by side.

slides.title: Dataform Best Practices
bullets: Design schema first — plan fields before building views
Create role-specific views rather than one view for everyone
Use data table for operations, gallery for browsing, sheet for bulk entry
Apply data filters to reduce noise — users see only relevant records
Set field permissions early — harder to change after go-live
Coordinate team edits — one editor at a time to avoid save conflicts
slides.notes: Close Dataforms section — this is often the most hands-on topic.
